records.title: Lifecycle validity and history integrity are separate coordination properties
records.summary: The project now distinguishes whether each peer-trigger receipt is structurally and temporally valid from whether the ordered receipt history has been truncated, reordered, mutated, omitted, or forked. R-007 adds ordered Merkle checkpoints as the second property and explicitly does not treat them as proof that an event claim is true.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Fetch the R-007 queue update, implementation commit, and test commit. Reject this record if the implementation does not domain-separate ordered leaves or the tests fail to reject at least truncation and prior-history mutation.

records.title: Coordination checkpoints are not yet independently witnessed
records.summary: The checkpoint roots are unsigned, require full-prefix verification, and have no independent witness or gossip mechanism. Two isolated clients could therefore retain different internally consistent histories without detecting the fork.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Resolve only after committed evidence shows compact consistency verification plus a signature, external anchor, or independent checkpoint exchange that detects divergent histories.

records.title: Canonical queue is stale relative to additive team update records
records.summary: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json ends its frontier history at R-004, while a committed additive queue-update record marks R-007 completed. The canonical file remains authoritative for listed items, but it is not a complete view of subsequent team work. Continuity readers must merge canonical state with provenance-preserving queue-update records rather than assuming absence means no work occurred.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Compare ACTIVE_QUEUE.json blob abb807e1a25d399f301238f6b8cc26d6be10385f with the R-007 update blob. Falsify this record if R-007 is present in that canonical queue version or the update file is not committed.

records.title: Continuity reconstruction must merge canonical snapshots with append-only deltas
records.summary: A reliable project-state reader should treat canonical state files as bounded snapshots and append-only team update files as later deltas, then produce a derived view with explicit source precedence and temporal ordering. This avoids both destructive canonical rewrites and false conclusions from stale snapshots.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Build a deterministic merge prototype and test it against conflicting timestamps, duplicate IDs, stale snapshots, and two deltas that claim incompatible terminal states.

records.title: A derived merged-state view is proposed but not implemented
records.summary: Create a dependency-free continuity projector that reads the canonical queue plus operations/queue-updates records, validates ownership and chronology, preserves all conflicting claims, and emits a derived non-authoritative state view. It must never modify canonical team records.
records.falsification_or_review_route: The proposal fails if the projector drops conflicts, overwrites source records, accepts another team's ownership mutation, or cannot reproduce its output deterministically from fixed inputs.
